name: ATTE
description: Quad unity-gain attenuator/inverter with cascaded input normalization · per-channel +/- switch flips between attenuator and inverter · IN A normalled to +10V so unpatched channels act as manual offset generators.

controls.name: A
controls.description: Manual attenuator for channel 1. Scales signal from zero (fully left) to unity, with precise zero at the CCW end.

controls.name: Mode switch (+/−)
controls.description: Per-channel switch scaling output by +1 (attenuator) or −1 (inverter).

inputs.name: IN A
inputs.description: Channel 1 signal input. When unpatched, normalled to an internal +10V reference so the channel acts as a manual offset generator from 0V to ±10V.

inputs.name: IN B
inputs.description: Channel 2 input. When unpatched, normalled from IN A so a signal at A splits across B, C, D until a jack breaks the chain.
inputs.normalizedTo: IN A signal when IN B unpatched

inputs.name: IN C
inputs.description: Channel 3 input. Normalled from IN B; the signal at the nearest patched jack above propagates down.
inputs.normalizedTo: IN B signal when IN C unpatched

inputs.name: IN D
inputs.description: Channel 4 input. Normalled from IN C. Patching D breaks the cascade at that point only.
inputs.normalizedTo: IN C signal when IN D unpatched

outputs.name: OUT A
outputs.description: Channel 1 scaled output. LED glows green for positive signal, red for inverted/negative signal.

outputs.name: OUT B
outputs.description: Channel 2 scaled output with bipolar LED indicator.

outputs.name: OUT C
outputs.description: Channel 3 scaled output with bipolar LED indicator.

outputs.name: OUT D
outputs.description: Channel 4 scaled output with bipolar LED indicator.

behaviors.name: Manual offset generator
behaviors.description: IN A normals to an internal +10V rail. Knob A now sweeps 0V to +10V, switch flipping to − flips to −10V to 0V. Channels B-D inherit this via the cascade, turning one empty ATTE into four independent manual offsets.

behaviors.name: 1-to-4 splitter with per-tap scaling
behaviors.trigger: signal patched to IN A only
behaviors.description: A at IN A normals into IN B, C and D. Each channel's knob scales its own copy so one source feeds four destinations at different levels — a VCO to four filter cutoffs, or an envelope to four VCAs with independent depth.

behaviors.name: Attenuverter per channel
behaviors.trigger: mode switch in − position with a signal on the input
behaviors.description: Flipping − inverts the polarity. Knob fully down is still a precise zero, so a typical attenuverter sweep crosses zero at the left stop rather than at centre — not a bipolar pot.

behaviors.name: Cascade break point
behaviors.trigger: patching IN B, C, or D
behaviors.description: Patching any IN jack stops the normal from propagating further down. Split the four channels into 1+3, 2+2, 3+1 or 4×1 groups by choosing which jacks to insert.

behaviors.name: Bipolar LED metering
behaviors.description: Each OUT has a dual-colour LED: green for positive, red for negative. Quick visual check for polarity when patching unfamiliar modulation sources or confirming an inverter switch did what you expected.

patchIdeas.name: Four manual offsets
patchIdeas.patch: All INs empty · IN A normalled to +10V propagates to B, C, D · each knob becomes a 0-10V offset · flip channel switches to − for negative offsets · feed four destinations that need static CV with no external module
patchIdeas.diagram:
ATTE:
* All INs: unpatched
* CH 1: + · knob sets offset
* CH 2: − · knob sets negative offset
* CH 3-4: + · taste

VCO 1:
VCO 2:
Filter:
VCA:

- ATTE (OUT A) >> VCO 1 (FM)
- ATTE (OUT B) >> VCO 2 (Detune)
- ATTE (OUT C) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- ATTE (OUT D) >> VCA (Bias)

patchIdeas.name: LFO splitter with depth per destination
patchIdeas.patch: LFO → IN A · cascade feeds B, C, D · each knob dials modulation depth independently · switch on one channel to − for out-of-phase modulation · one source modulates four parameters at different intensities and polarities
patchIdeas.diagram:
ATTE:
* CH 1-3: + · depth to taste
* CH 4: − · inverted copy

LFO:
VCO:
Filter:
VCA:
Pan:

- LFO (Out) >> ATTE (IN A)
- ATTE (OUT A) >> VCO (FM)
- ATTE (OUT B) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- ATTE (OUT C) >> VCA (CV)
- ATTE (OUT D) >> Pan (CV) // inverted

patchIdeas.name: Attenuverter for bipolar CV
patchIdeas.patch: Bipolar CV (±5V) → IN A · switch to − to send −5..+5 inverted · knob scales 0-100% of the signal · zero at full CCW makes muting precise · useful for taming a hot source or flipping a modulation polarity
patchIdeas.diagram:
ATTE:
* CH 1: switch − · knob = depth

LFO:
Filter:

- LFO (Out) >> ATTE (IN A)
- ATTE (OUT A) >> Filter (Cutoff) // inverted, attenuated

patchIdeas.name: Two stereo attenuators (2+2 split)
patchIdeas.patch: Patch L and R of signal pair one into IN A and IN B (breaks cascade at B) · signal pair two into IN C and IN D · knobs A+B act as linked stereo level for pair one, C+D for pair two · true stereo two-channel sub-mixer
patchIdeas.diagram:
ATTE:
* CH 1-2: pair 1 level
* CH 3-4: pair 2 level

Reverb:
Delay:
Mixer:

- Reverb (Out L) -> ATTE (IN A)
- Reverb (Out R) -> ATTE (IN B) // break cascade
- Delay (Out L) -> ATTE (IN C)
- Delay (Out R) -> ATTE (IN D)
- ATTE (OUT A) -> Mixer (In L1)
- ATTE (OUT B) -> Mixer (In R1)
- ATTE (OUT C) -> Mixer (In L2)
- ATTE (OUT D) -> Mixer (In R2)

patchIdeas.name: Envelope + manual offset bias
patchIdeas.patch: Envelope → IN A (breaks cascade to CH 1 only) · IN B unpatched inherits +10V · knob B becomes a bias offset · sum envelope and offset externally at a filter cutoff for a floor-plus-envelope shape · add two more offsets on C and D
patchIdeas.diagram:
ATTE:
* CH 1: + · envelope depth
* CH 2: + · bias offset

Envelope:
Mixer:
Filter:

- Envelope (Out) >> ATTE (IN A)
- ATTE (OUT A) >> Mixer (In 1) // envelope scaled
- ATTE (OUT B) >> Mixer (In 2) // +10V × knob B
- Mixer (Sum) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Inverted VCO for through-zero FM
patchIdeas.patch: VCO → IN A · switch to − · knob dials modulation index · OUT A → FM input of another VCO · adding a second ATTE channel (+) of the same source to a separate FM bus gives simultaneous upper- and lower-sideband weighting
patchIdeas.diagram:
ATTE:
* CH 1: − · FM depth
* CH 2: + · FM depth on second VCO

VCO A:
VCO B:
VCO C:

- VCO A (Out) -> ATTE (IN A)
- ATTE (OUT A) -> VCO B (FM) // inverted
- ATTE (OUT B) -> VCO C (FM) // positive
